info.description: Personal nutrition tracking REST API. All endpoints under /products, /meals, and /summary require a bearer token.
info.title: Nutrition API

get.description: One read that returns the day's adherence + nutrition totals + hydration ml + workouts + workout-fuel entries + body-weight state + training-phase context + goal-override presence. Composition-only over existing primitives — use this as the first call of a session; for deep dives into one slice, use the dedicated tools (daily_summary, list_workouts, etc.) which include the per-entry detail this aggregator omits.
get.summary: Daily context bundle

get.description: Per-day EA values + window aggregate, computed from meals (intake), workouts (exercise burn) and body weight (composition). Loucks bands: `< 30 kcal/kg FFM/day = low`, `30-45 = sub_optimal`, `>= 45 = adequate`. FFM resolution order: lean_mass_kg query param → body_fat_pct query param + stored body weight → most-recent in-window stored body_fat_pct → 85% fallback (loud, flagged via `composition.composition_estimated`). Days with workouts missing `kcal_burned` are listed in `missing_burn_workout_ids` and excluded from `window.avg_ea`.
get.summary: Energy Availability over a window

parameters.description: Exclusive RFC3339 upper bound; max 92 days from 'from'
parameters.name: to

parameters.description: IANA timezone for calendar-day boundaries; defaults to DEFAULT_USER_TZ
parameters.name: tz

400.description: window_required | window_invalid | range_too_large | tz_invalid | lean_mass_kg_invalid | body_fat_pct_invalid | weight_data_missing

get.summary: List all goal templates
Get a named goal template

put.description: Full-replace semantics: absent nutrient bounds are stored as NULL. The template's `name` is the URL path segment (kebab-case-ish, user-chosen). `Idempotency-Key` is NOT accepted on PUT.

400.description: template_name_invalid | template_name_too_long | goal_value_invalid | goal_range_invalid | invalid_json | idempotency_unsupported_for_put

delete.description: Refused with 409 template_in_use if any phase references the template via default_template_id.

get.summary: Get current nutrition goals

put.description: Full-replace semantics: absent fields are stored as NULL (cleared). Each goal is `{min?, max?}` with at least one bound; ranges must satisfy min <= max. Note: `Idempotency-Key` is NOT accepted on PUT — supplying the header returns `400 idempotency_unsupported_for_put`. ETag/If-Match retry-safety is forward-pointed but not implemented.

get.description: Returns overrides whose `date` falls within `[from, to]` (inclusive). Dates without an override are omitted (the caller can infer the default applies).
get.summary: List daily goal overrides in a date range

parameters.description: Inclusive end date YYYY-MM-DD; max 366 days from `from`
parameters.name: to

get.summary: Get a daily goal override for a specific date

put.description: Full-replace semantics: absent fields are stored as NULL. Same validation as PUT /goals. `Idempotency-Key` is NOT accepted on PUT — supplying it returns `400 idempotency_unsupported_for_put`.

get.summary: List hydration entries in a window

post.description: Records a volume of fluid drunk at a moment in time. The optional `note` carries beverage context (e.g. "water", "coffee", "electrolytes").

get.summary: List meal entries in a time window

post.summary: Log a meal entry against an existing product

post.description: Use when the user is logging a meal that doesn't map to a barcode. Optionally persists the item as a manual product via `save_as_product`.
post.summary: Log a freeform meal entry (no product lookup)

post.description: Accepts a `multipart/form-data` upload with an `image` part (JPEG or PNG; HEIC is rejected with 415 in v1 because we don't ship libheif). The backend resizes the image to a max edge of 1568px, sends it to Claude Vision with a tool-forced output, and uses the returned `{name, nutriments_per_100g}` to create a freeform meal entry. The response carries both the canonical `meal` block and an `inference` block reporting model, confidence, token usage, and image dimensions. Returns `503 vision_unavailable` when `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` is not configured.
post.summary: Log a meal from a photo via Claude Vision

parameters.description: Optional client-supplied idempotency key. Hash includes the raw image bytes so byte-identical replays return the original meal without a second Claude call.
parameters.name: Idempotency-Key

parameters.description: Image file (JPEG or PNG; HEIC rejected in v1).
parameters.name: image

400.description: image_required | quantity_g_invalid | logged_at_invalid | logged_at_too_far_future | meal_type_invalid | note_too_long

get.summary: Get a meal entry by ID

parameters.description: Set to `components` to include recipe breakdown when the meal's product is a recipe
parameters.name: expand

patch.description: Unknown fields are ignored. Send only the keys you want to change.
patch.summary: Update fields on a meal entry

get.summary: List training phases intersecting a date window

parameters.description: Inclusive end date YYYY-MM-DD; max 730 days from from
parameters.name: to

post.description: A phase is a named date range tagged with a training type. Optionally points at a goal template via default_template_id, which becomes the default daily goals for every date in [start_date, end_date]. Per-date overrides still win.

400.description: phase_name_invalid | phase_name_too_long | phase_type_invalid | date_range_invalid | date_invalid | invalid_json | template_not_found

get.summary: Get a training phase by id

patch.description: Tri-state on default_template_id: empty string clears, UUID sets, missing leaves unchanged.

400.description: phase_id_invalid | invalid_json | patch_empty | phase_name_invalid | phase_name_too_long | phase_type_invalid | date_invalid | date_range_invalid | default_template_id_invalid | template_not_found

get.description: Returns the product cache as a paginated list, ordered last_logged_at desc then name asc. Combine with `delete` to clean up leftovers.
get.summary: List products with pagination

parameters.description: Filter by source: off | manual | recipe
parameters.name: source

post.description: Creates a product not sourced from Open Food Facts. Default source is "manual". Pass `source: "recipe"` together with an `external_url` to register a flat-imported recipe (e.g. from the Cookidoo Chrome extension); the row has no `product_components` and `nutriment_computed_at` stays null — distinguishing it from composed recipes built via POST /products/recipes.
post.summary: Create a product (manual or flat-imported recipe)

400.description: invalid_json | name_required | source_invalid | external_url_too_long | external_url_invalid | nutriments_invalid

post.description: Sibling route to POST /products/lookup/{barcode}. Returns 400 when the path parameter is missing instead of falling through to a generic 404.
post.summary: Reject barcode lookup with no barcode

post.description: Fetches a product from the local cache or upstream Open Food Facts. Pass `refresh=true` to force an upstream re-fetch.
post.summary: Look up a product by barcode

parameters.description: Force a fresh OFF fetch even if cached
parameters.name: refresh

post.description: Builds a recipe from N component products and per-component grams. Nutriments-per-100g are computed at creation time from components, so existing meal-logging math works unchanged.

post.summary: Recompute a recipe's nutriments from its current components

get.description: Case-insensitive substring match across product name and brand. Returns up to 50 results.
get.summary: Search products
Get a product by ID

parameters.description: Set to `components` to include a recipe's components
parameters.name: expand

delete.description: Hard-deletes a product. Historical meal_entries get a snapshot of the product's current name + nutriments before the FK is nulled, so daily/range summaries continue to reflect those meals. If the product is referenced as a component of any recipe, the request is rejected with 409 listing the using recipes — delete or replace within those recipes first.

409.description: product_in_use_as_component (body includes the using recipes list and a hint)

get.description: Stateless, deterministic carb-load math: given a race date and a body weight, returns daily carbohydrate targets for the load window and race morning. No persistence. The natural follow-up is to translate each entry into a goal override via `PUT /goals/overrides/{date}`.
get.summary: Compute a carb-load schedule for a race

parameters.description: Race date YYYY-MM-DD (must be today or later in DEFAULT_USER_TZ)
parameters.name: race_date

400.description: race_date_required | body_weight_kg_required | race_date_invalid | race_date_in_past | body_weight_kg_invalid | days_before_invalid | carbs_per_kg_per_day_invalid | race_day_carbs_per_kg_invalid

post.description: Same inputs as `GET /race-prep/carb-load`. Computes the schedule, then in a single transaction writes the per-day carb target into the `daily_goal_overrides` row for each schedule date — merging into existing overrides (preserving non-carb fields) or creating new rows. Returns the schedule plus a per-date `applied` outcome (`{date, carbs_g_min, created}`). On any per-date failure the whole transaction rolls back and zero overrides are persisted.
post.summary: Compute AND apply a carb-load schedule into per-date goal overrides

parameters.description: Carb-load parameters (same as the GET endpoint)
parameters.name: body

400.description: race_date_required | body_weight_kg_required | race_date_invalid | race_date_in_past | body_weight_kg_invalid | days_before_invalid | carbs_per_kg_per_day_invalid | race_day_carbs_per_kg_invalid | invalid_json

get.description: Stateless literature-grounded fueling recommendation for one training or race session. Accepts EITHER `workout_id` (pulls sport/duration/intensity from the row, deriving intensity zone from `tss` when present) OR the explicit triplet `sport`+`duration_min`+`intensity_zone`. Body weight resolved via the four-tier rule (explicit override > rolling 7d stored mean > most-recent stored entry > 400). Reuses the 0.3 g/kg MPS threshold from `protein_distribution` for the post-workout protein recommendation.
get.summary: Pre/intra/post fueling recommendation for a single session

parameters.description: Workout UUID — pulls sport/duration/intensity from the row
parameters.name: workout_id

parameters.description: Duration in minutes (>0). Required in explicit mode.
parameters.name: duration_min

parameters.description: Explicit body-weight override (>0). Otherwise resolved from stored entries.
parameters.name: body_weight_kg

400.description: input_required | input_conflict | sport_required | duration_min_required | intensity_zone_required | sport_invalid | duration_min_invalid | intensity_zone_invalid | body_weight_kg_invalid | weight_data_missing | workout_id_invalid

get.description: Returns aggregated totals for a single calendar date in the supplied timezone. When `meal_type` is set, only entries of that type are summed and adherence is omitted.
get.summary: Daily nutrition totals

get.description: Returns total ml and per-entry list for one calendar date in the supplied timezone. Volume-only — separate from /summary/daily, which is nutrient-only.
get.summary: Daily hydration total + entries

get.description: Returns one row per `meal_entries` row on `date`, annotated with `mps_effective: bool` (against the 0.3 g/kg body-weight muscle-protein-synthesis threshold) plus `logged_at_hour` and `gap_minutes_since_previous` for circadian + meal-spacing context. The headline metric is `mps_effective_meal_count / meal_count`. Body weight resolution order: explicit `body_weight_kg` query param > rolling 7-day mean of stored entries ending at `date` (inclusive) > most-recent stored entry strictly before `date`. With no stored data and no override, returns `400 weight_data_missing`.
get.summary: Per-meal protein distribution with MPS-threshold annotations

get.description: Returns one row per day in `[from, to]`. Range is capped at 92 days inclusive.
get.summary: Daily totals across a date range

get.description: Returns the trailing-window average of nutrition totals as of `anchor_date`. The window is `[anchor_date − (window_days − 1) days, anchor_date]`, both inclusive, in the requested `tz`. **Averages are computed across days with logged meals (`days_with_data`), NOT across `total_days`** — a 7-day window with 5 logged days returns the 5-day mean, with both divisors exposed so a sparse window is loud. Per-day rows carry `has_data: bool` so "no meals logged" stays distinct from "logged zero." Adherence is computed against the goal resolved at `anchor_date` (honoring per-date overrides). `window_days` is bounded `[2, 30]`.
get.summary: Rolling-window nutrition average

parameters.description: Calendar date in YYYY-MM-DD (the trailing window ends here)
parameters.name: anchor_date

parameters.description: Window size in calendar days; range [2, 30]
parameters.name: window_days

get.summary: List body-weight entries in a window

parameters.description: Exclusive RFC3339 upper bound; max 92-day span
parameters.name: to

post.description: Records a body weight (kg), optionally with body-fat %. Multiple measurements per day are allowed and smoothed by /weight/trend. Standard `Idempotency-Key` header supported.

get.description: Returns one point per calendar date in [from, to], each carrying the trailing rolling average over `window_days` days and the `sample_count` that fed it. A `sample_count` of 0 means no data in the trailing window; the matching `rolling_avg_kg` is null.
get.summary: Rolling-average body-weight trend

parameters.description: Inclusive end date YYYY-MM-DD; max 366-day span
parameters.name: to

400.description: range_required | date_invalid | range_invalid | range_too_large | window_days_invalid | tz_invalid

get.summary: List workout-fuel entries in a window

post.description: Records an in-session fueling event — gel, electrolyte drink, salt tab, caffeine pill. At least one of quantity_ml/carbs_g/sodium_mg/potassium_mg/caffeine_mg MUST be set. `caffeine_mg: 0` means "measured, no caffeine"; omitting means "not measured" (NULL). Plain water belongs in /hydration; anything with electrolytes/carbs/caffeine belongs here.

400.description: name_required | empty_entry | quantity_ml_invalid | carbs_g_invalid | sodium_mg_invalid | potassium_mg_invalid | caffeine_mg_invalid | logged_at_invalid | logged_at_too_far_future | note_too_long | workout_id_invalid | workout_not_found

patch.description: Any field may be sent. For nullable fields, an explicit JSON `null` clears the column (sets it to NULL); omitting the field leaves it untouched. `workout_id` additionally honours the empty-string clear sentinel ("") for consistency with meals/hydration. PATCH that would leave all five quantitative fields null is rejected as `empty_entry`.

400.description: name_required | empty_entry | quantity_ml_invalid | carbs_g_invalid | sodium_mg_invalid | potassium_mg_invalid | caffeine_mg_invalid | logged_at_invalid | logged_at_too_far_future | note_too_long | workout_id_invalid | workout_not_found

get.summary: List workouts in a time window

post.description: Creates a new workout when external_id is absent or unseen; updates the existing row when external_id matches an existing workout. Garmin re-syncs land here without the writer needing to track sync state.

parameters.description: Optional client-supplied idempotency key (writers normally rely on external_id instead)
parameters.name: Idempotency-Key

400.description: source_invalid | sport_invalid | window_invalid | started_at_too_far_future | kcal_burned_invalid | avg_hr_invalid | tss_invalid

post.description: Each item upserts independently using external_id semantics. Per-item validation/persistence failures are reported in the results array; the overall response is 200 whenever the request body is well-formed and within the size cap. Partial failure is allowed.
post.summary: Upsert a batch of workouts (max 100 per request)

get.summary: Get a workout by id

patch.description: Accepts `name`, `notes`, `kcal_burned`, `avg_hr`, `tss`. `source`, `external_id`, `sport`, `started_at`, `ended_at` are immutable — delete and re-create if those are wrong.
patch.summary: Partially update a workout (mutable fields only)

get.description: Returns three time-anchored buckets (pre / intra / post), each carrying three separate sub-objects of intake around a workout: `nutrition` (from meals), `hydration` (from hydration entries), and `workout_fuel` (from workout-fuel entries — gels / electrolyte drinks / caffeine). Aggregation is by `logged_at` time-window matching, NOT by the `workout_id` tag on intake rows — an untagged meal in the pre-window still contributes. Sub-objects are unit-isolated: no ml inside `nutrition.totals`, no kcal inside `hydration` or `workout_fuel`.
get.summary: Workout fueling: pre / intra / post windows

phase.description: nil when no phase covers the date

weight.description: nil when no entry ever logged

workout_fuel.description: never nil; empty array on quiet days

workouts.description: never nil; empty array on quiet days

workout_id.description:
WorkoutID supports the empty-string clear sentinel:
  omitted   → leave unchanged
  "<uuid>"  → set the link
  ""        → clear the link

WorkoutID supports the empty-string sentinel for clear:
  omitted   → leave unchanged
  "<uuid>"  → set the link
  ""        → clear the link

external_url.description:
ExternalURL records where this product came from when neither OFF nor a
manual user entry. Today's first user is the Cookidoo extension.

last_logged_quantity_g.description:
LastLoggedQuantityG is the quantity_g of the most recent meal entry that
advanced last_logged_at. The phone's scan→log flow defaults to this
value, dropping a typical scan from 3 taps to 2. Lockstep with
LastLoggedAt — see internal/products.Repo.TouchLastLoggedAt.

nutriment_computed_at.description:
NutrimentComputedAt is set when a recipe product's nutriments were derived
from its components. Null for non-recipe products.

external_url.description:
ExternalURL records the upstream page this product was imported from.
Optional; max 2048 chars; trimmed before validation.

source.description:
Source defaults to "manual". Accepts "manual" or "recipe" — the latter
is the flat-imported-recipe path (e.g. the Cookidoo Chrome extension).

external_url.description:
ExternalURL records where this product came from when neither OFF nor a
manual user entry. Today's first user is the Cookidoo extension.

last_logged_quantity_g.description:
LastLoggedQuantityG is the quantity_g of the most recent meal entry that
advanced last_logged_at. The phone's scan→log flow defaults to this
value, dropping a typical scan from 3 taps to 2. Lockstep with
LastLoggedAt — see internal/products.Repo.TouchLastLoggedAt.

nutriment_computed_at.description:
NutrimentComputedAt is set when a recipe product's nutriments were derived
from its components. Null for non-recipe products.

days_before.description: 0 for race day, N for N days before

rpe.description:
Rehearsal-outcome signals echoed from the underlying workout row when
set. Lets the agent read "perceived effort + GI + fueling totals" in
one call — the natural rehearsal-evaluation shape.

Per-session rehearsal-outcome signals — both nullable, set by the user
after a fueling-rehearsal workout. Validated 1..10 (Borg CR-10) and 1..5
(1=no GI distress, 5=severe) at handler + DB CHECK layers.

BearerAuth.description: Static bearer token. Use the format `Bearer <token>` with either MOBILE_API_TOKEN or AGENT_API_TOKEN.
BearerAuth.name: Authorization
